commitMessage:
docs: add CLAUDE.md with posting-to-writing-API instructions

Documents env vars, one-time login flow, and a subprocess helper
function for ideas-bot to create posts on quixotry.me headlessly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.summary: Container App Job triggered by ideas-api. Clones the target repository, runs an AI-driven tool-use loop to implement the requested feature, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request on GitHub. The job scales to zero between invocations.
content.description: A containerised Python agent that autonomously implements feature ideas. Triggered on demand by ideas-api, it clones the target repo, runs a GPT-4o tool-use loop to read/write files and execute shell commands, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request.
features: App-level retry mechanism for GPT-4o API calls with up to 4 attempts and exponential backoff (60/120/300s) to handle sustained 429 rate limits
Sliding context window for GPT-4o tool-use loop now trims older exchanges beyond the last 14 to reduce token growth and further mitigate 429 risks
Decomposes complex ideas into 2–4 focused sub-tasks, each assigned to the correct project based on repo-specific work, reducing token usage and API load
Sub-tasks are now ordered and can be chained with hard dependencies, ensuring backend tasks complete before dependent frontend tasks
Reduced token caps for tool outputs and agent loops: bash output capped at 2500 chars, read_file capped at 4000 chars, and max_tokens reduced to 4096
Fetches CLAUDE.md from the target repository before the clarity check and agent execution, injecting repo-specific context to improve decision-making and reduce unnecessary questions
Prior Q&A from previous triggers is now fetched and injected into the clarity check, preventing repeated questions and improving efficiency

Added query_table tool for read-only access to Azure Table Storage, enabling the agent to inspect live ideas, projects, and updates tables to validate assumptions and understand record shapes before writing code
Increased agent round limit from 40 to 60, with a wrap-up nudge injected at round 50 to preserve partial work and ensure progress is committed before hitting the limit
Agent now surfaces a summary explanation when no commits are made, allowing users to understand why no changes were implemented and providing actionable feedback

architecture.overview: A containerised Python agent that autonomously implements feature ideas. Triggered on demand by ideas-api, it clones the target repo, performs a pre-flight clarity check, runs an AI-driven tool-use loop to read/write files and execute shell commands, commits the result, and opens a draft pull request.
keyPoints: GPT-4o tool-use loop now includes app-level retry mechanism with up to 4 attempts and exponential backoff (60/120/300s) for handling sustained 429 rate limits
Sliding context window trims older exchanges beyond the last 14 to bound token usage and reduce 429 risks
Idea decomposition mechanism splits complex ideas into 2–4 focused sub-tasks, each assigned to the correct project based on repo-specific work, blocking the parent idea until all sub-tasks complete
Sub-tasks are now ordered and can be chained with hard dependencies by setting 'depends_on_previous': true, ensuring backend tasks complete before dependent frontend tasks
Token caps adjusted to reduce API load: bash output capped at 2500 chars, read_file capped at 4000 chars, and max_tokens reduced to 4096 in agent and repair loops

CLAUDE.md from the target repository is fetched and injected into the clarity check and agent system prompt, enabling the bot to answer structural questions autonomously and start with architecture context
Prior Q&A from previous triggers is now injected into the clarity check, ensuring previously resolved questions are not re-asked and improving clarity assessment efficiency

New query_table tool provides read-only access to Azure Table Storage, allowing the agent to inspect live data in ideas, projects, and updates tables to validate assumptions and understand record shapes before writing code
Agent round limit increased from 40 to 60, with a wrap-up nudge injected at round 50 to commit partial work and summarize progress, ensuring no work is lost if the hard cap is reached
Agent now returns a summary explanation when no commits are made, enabling actionable feedback to users and improving transparency in cases where no changes are implemented

dataFlow.label: Decompose complex ideas into sub-tasks if needed · block parent idea
sublines: Evaluate idea complexity · create 2–4 focused sub-tasks
Assign exact project_name per subtask based on repo-specific work
Order sub-tasks and chain dependencies if needed (e.g., backend before frontend)
File sub-tasks via Ideas API · update parent idea status
